task:
ROOT-CAUSE AND FIX Issue 1: a brand-new workspace's FIRST run launches the claude CLI with `--model opus` and fails 'model opus not available', even though the persona's assigned model is the empty-string default.

WHAT IS ALREADY VERIFIED (do not re-litigate or change these — they are correct):
- The seeded template `templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/personas/assignments.json` uses `"model": ""` for every persona (empty = default).
- `ClaudeAdapter.build()` (`packages/adapters/src/claude.ts:32` headless, `:39` interactive) correctly skips `--model` when `input.model` is falsy.
- `resolvePlayAssignment` / `resolveEffectivePersona` / `loadAssignments` pass an empty model through UNCHANGED (confirmed by trace).

SO THE DEFECT IS UPSTREAM on the new-workspace launch path. Find the EXACT place where an empty/'default' model becomes the concrete alias `opus` and is passed to the adapter `build()` for a fresh-workspace first launch. Strong suspects (investigate and prove which one, with file:line evidence, before editing):
  1. The UI persona/launch save path: `adaptPersonas` converts `'' -> 'Default'` for DISPLAY (`adapter.ts:480`) and `personasToAssignments` converts `'Default' -> ''` on SAVE (`adapter.ts:487,495-502`). Check EVERY save/launch path actually round-trips through that conversion — a path that persists the displayed value, or a model dropdown that defaults to a concrete alias (`models[0]` = `opus`), would write/launch `opus`.
  2. Any model-dropdown default / launch-modal default that picks the first enumerable model (`listModels()` returns `['opus', ...]`).
  3. Any fallback/coalesce in `packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts` (`assembleRunInput`, run-input assembly) or `POST /runs` in `packages/daemon/src/routes.ts` that substitutes a concrete model when the assignment is empty.
  4. `top-tier` resolution (`resolveTopTier` / `createDaemonTopTierResolver`) being hit for an ordinary persona launch with an empty model.

FIX at the ROOT so 'default' passes through as NO model end-to-end: when a persona's model is the empty-string default, NO `--model` flag is ever produced and the CLI uses its own configured default. Do NOT paper over it by special-casing `opus` in the adapter.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (must all hold):
- A brand-new workspace's first-run launch builds the claude argv with NO `--model` flag when the persona's model is the default (empty). Prove this with a regression test that exercises the real launch/build path (not just a unit test of `build()` in isolation — pin the actual fresh-workspace -> resolved persona -> built argv chain) and asserts the argv contains no `--model`.
- An explicitly pinned model (e.g. a persona assigned `"sonnet"`) STILL passes `--model sonnet` — do not break intentional pins.
- State in your handoff the exact file:line that was injecting `opus` and why the fresh-workspace path hit it.

MUST NOT BREAK: ordinary priority runs unchanged; `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm -w typecheck`, and `node scripts/check-topology.mjs` all green. Report the commands you ran and their results.

Do NOT touch Issue 2 (CLI-test model validation) — that is the next atom. Stay scoped to root-causing and fixing the empty-default -> opus injection plus its regression test.

RE-DO Issue 1 (the previous attempt was REJECTED for doing none of the work and churning ~30 unrelated files). Fix: a brand-new workspace's FIRST run launches the claude CLI with `--model opus` and fails 'model opus not available', even though the persona's assigned model is the empty-string default.

STEP 0 — CLEAN SLATE (mandatory). The working tree currently holds the previous rejected attempt: a `.ts`->`.js` import-extension sweep across ~30 files in packages/ui, a `seed.json` import-assertion change, and unrelated test edits in packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts, packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts, packages/core/tests/priority-authoring-plays.test.ts, and packages/ui/tests/adapter.test.ts. Restore ALL of those to their committed HEAD state (`git restore` / `git checkout -- <paths>`) so the working tree matches HEAD before you begin. Your FINAL `git diff` must contain ONLY the files you intentionally change for Issue 1. Do NOT rewrite import extensions and do NOT touch unrelated UI files — that churn is out of scope and will be rejected again.

STEP 1 — REPRODUCE FIRST (this also produces the required regression test). Write a test that drives the REAL fresh-workspace launch path end to end: create/scaffold a fresh workspace (the createWorkspace/scaffoldCocoderZone path, OR assembleRunInput against a freshly-scaffolded `cocoder/personas/assignments.json` that has `"model": ""`), resolve the personas, build the claude launch command through the adapter, and assert the built argv contains NO `--model` flag. Run it and OBSERVE the actual model string being injected — that reveals the true source.

ALREADY-VERIFIED CONTEXT (do not re-investigate or change these; they are correct for the empty case): empty-string assignment is passed through unchanged by resolveEffectivePersona / resolvePlayAssignment / assembleRunInput (launcher.ts:115-117); ClaudeAdapter.build skips `--model` when model is falsy (claude.ts:32,39); the UI adapter round-trips ''<->'Default' correctly (adapter.ts:482 display, :489/:502 save); routes.ts:382 preserves empty via `?? null`. So the concrete `opus` must enter from a NARROWER source — most likely one of: (a) the workspace's assignments.json actually persisting a concrete model (check exactly what createWorkspace / scaffoldCocoderZone seeds, and whether ANY path writes a model on workspace create), (b) a dropdown/launch path that selects `models[0]` (= 'opus' from listModels), or (c) a `top-tier`/resolveTopTier resolution hit for an ordinary persona launch. PROVE which one with file:line evidence before editing.

STEP 2 — FIX AT THE ROOT so 'default' passes through as NO model end-to-end: when a persona's model is the empty-string default, NO `--model` flag is ever produced and the CLI uses its own configured default. Do NOT paper over it by special-casing `opus` in the adapter.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (all must hold):
- The fresh-workspace first-run launch builds the claude argv with NO `--model` flag when the persona model is the default (empty), proven by the STEP-1 regression test on the real launch chain.
- An explicitly pinned model (a persona assigned e.g. `"sonnet"`) STILL produces `--model sonnet` — do not break intentional pins; add/keep a test asserting this.
- In your handoff, state the EXACT file:line that was injecting `opus` and WHY the fresh-workspace path hit it.
- Final `git diff` is limited to the Issue-1 root-cause fix + its regression test(s); nothing else.

MUST NOT BREAK: ordinary priority runs unchanged; `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui test`, `pnpm -w typecheck`, and `node scripts/check-topology.mjs` all green. Report each command you ran and its result.

Do NOT touch Issue 2 (CLI-test model validation) — that is the next atom.

BUILD Issue 2: make the CLI 'test' exercise the REAL model form so an unavailable model (INCLUDING the CLI's own configured default) fails at TEST time, not on the founder's first live run.

CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSE (Issue 1 is NOT a CoCoder code bug — do not try to fix the launch path): the founder's first-run 'model opus not available' came from the claude CLI's OWN user default (`~/.claude/settings.json` -> `"model": "opus[1m]"`). CoCoder correctly passes NO `--model` for a default persona (proven 3 ways), so the CLI uses its own default `opus[1m]`, which is unavailable. The gap: `ClaudeAdapter.preflight` only RECORDS the model string (`packages/adapters/src/claude.ts:63-65`, `checks.push({name:'model', ok:true, detail: model || '(claude default)'})`) and NEVER exercises it, so `testCli` reports claude green while its real default is broken.

STEP 0 — CLEAN SLATE: restore the working tree to HEAD (`git restore`/`git checkout -- .`) so ALL prior rejected-attempt changes are gone before you start. Your final `git diff` may touch ONLY the allowlisted files below.

FIX:
1. `packages/adapters/src/claude.ts` — make `preflight(model)` ACTUALLY exercise the model via a minimal real headless probe using the EXACT launch form `build()` produces: run through the injected `this.#exec` `claude -p [--model <model> ONLY when model is non-empty] "<tiny prompt, e.g. reply with the single word ok>"`. The `model` check is `ok` iff the probe exits 0; on non-zero, `ok:false` with a CLEAR detail derived from the CLI's stderr/stdout (surface the 'model ... not available' message). Only run the probe AFTER install+auth pass (don't probe an uninstalled/unauthed CLI; keep those as the failing checks). For `model===''` the probe runs with NO `--model` so it exercises the CLI's OWN configured default — this is what catches the founder's `opus[1m]` case. Update the now-stale `claude.ts:63-64` comment ('recorded, not hard-checked ... Phase-2 Oz feature') to reflect that preflight now performs the ADR-0006 §4 capability probe per founder direction (Issue 2).
2. `packages/daemon/src/clis.ts` — verify `testCli` now surfaces the failure: base `preflight('')` exercises the CLI's own default (the opus[1m] case) and its model failure must make the overall `entry.preflight.ok` false; the existing `configuredModels` loop exercises pinned models. Make ONLY a minimal wiring change if genuinely required; if the existing structure already surfaces it, change nothing here.
3. `packages/adapters/tests/adapters.test.ts` — add tests using the injected fake `Exec` (ClaudeAdapter ctor takes `exec`): (a) default probe SUCCESS (fake returns code 0 for `claude -p` with NO --model) -> model check ok; (b) default probe FAILURE (fake returns non-zero 'model opus not available' for `claude -p` with NO --model) -> model check ok:false with that detail AND `preflight('').ok === false`; (c) pinned probe uses the `--model <model>` form and fails with a clear detail when unavailable; (d) the model probe runs only after install+auth pass.
4. `packages/daemon/tests/fresh-workspace-model-launch.test.ts` (NEW) — re-add the GOOD Issue-1 regression test from the prior attempt VERBATIM (fresh-scaffolded workspace: default persona -> claude argv has NO `--model`; explicit `sonnet` pin -> `--model sonnet`). This closes Issue 1 as 'code already correct, now guarded'. Bring back NOTHING else from the rejected attempt (no validFounderCloseout/helpers, no claudeExecOk, no ui adapter.test.ts edit, no `.ts`->`.js` import churn).

HARD SCOPE — the final `git diff` may touch ONLY these four paths: `packages/adapters/src/claude.ts`, `packages/adapters/tests/adapters.test.ts`, `packages/daemon/src/clis.ts` (only if minimally required), `packages/daemon/tests/fresh-workspace-model-launch.test.ts`. Do NOT touch the tier-resolution surface (`resolveDeepReadAssignments`/`modelPin`/`resolveTopTier`) — that is owned by the first-class-model-tiers priority; do NOT create a parallel model-tier contract.

ACCEPTANCE: testing the claude CLI exercises the real model-arg form and FAILS (with a clear detail) when the configured/aliased model OR the CLI's own default is unavailable; an available model/default passes; the fresh-workspace test proves default->no `--model` and pin->`--model sonnet`. In your handoff, name the exact behavior change in `preflight` and confirm the diff is limited to the four allowlisted paths.

MUST NOT BREAK: `pnpm --filter @cocoder/adapters test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui test`, `pnpm -w typecheck`, and `node scripts/check-topology.mjs` all green. Report each command you ran and its result.

REPAIR MAIN, part 1 of 2 (founder-authorized): fix the 11 PRE-EXISTING test-suite failures on main so `@cocoder/core test`, `@cocoder/ui test`, and `@cocoder/daemon test` are all green. These are NOT caused by recent run_160 work — they are stale consumers/tests left behind by shipped contracts from earlier runs. The fix in every case is to CONFORM the consumer/test to the intended shipped contract — do NOT revert the shipped behavior. (The 31 UI typecheck errors are a SEPARATE, more delicate atom — do NOT touch tsconfig/import-extensions/JSON-attributes here.)

STEP 0 — start from a clean tree (current HEAD is `930d52b`; `git status` should show no changes). Your final diff must be limited to the allowlist below.

FRONT A — DAEMON (6 failures in `packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts` and `packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts`): the run-lifecycle wrap-up now validates the founder-closeout FORMAT (validator in `packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts`; the authoritative format is owned by `packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md` — the 'Founder Completion Brief' with sections like **Atom Complete**, **Run Status**, **Recommended Next Step**, etc.). The failing tests fake `runHeadless`/io wrap output as the placeholder string `'wrap closeout'` / `'no changes'`, which the validator now rejects, so runs end 'failed' instead of 'completed'. FIX: add a small shared test helper `packages/daemon/tests/helpers/founder-closeout.ts` exporting a `validFounderCloseout(...)` that returns a string SATISFYING the real validator (derive the exact required sections from the validator + wrap-up.md — do not guess), and replace the placeholder wrap outputs in the failing tests so the lifecycle reaches 'completed'. Do NOT weaken or change the validator.

FRONT B — UI (4 failures in `packages/ui/tests/adapter.test.ts` 'clis > ...'): the daemon CliView gained a `model` check field (it is consumed at `packages/ui/src/renderer/adapter.ts` ~line 91-92 via `view.model.ok`). The ui `clis` mapping tests/fixtures don't provide/handle `model`. FIX: update the ui CLI adapter mapping (`packages/ui/src/renderer/adapter.ts`) and the test fixture (`packages/ui/tests/adapter.test.ts`) so a `model` CliCheckView is handled and the four `clis` mapping tests pass. Conform the consumer to the new CliView shape; do not strip the `model` field.

FRONT C — CORE (1 failure in `packages/core/tests/priority-authoring-plays.test.ts` 'Architect Play System priority includes elegance checkpoint contract migration'): the test reads `cocoder/priorities/hybrid-plays.md`, but that priority was ARCHIVED to `cocoder/priorities/archive/hybrid-plays.md` (the asserted strings still exist there). FIX: update the test to read the archived path so the contract assertion still holds. Do NOT un-archive the priority.

HARD SCOPE — final `git diff` may touch ONLY: `packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts`, `packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts`, `packages/daemon/tests/helpers/founder-closeout.ts` (new), `packages/ui/src/renderer/adapter.ts`, `packages/ui/tests/adapter.test.ts`, `packages/core/tests/priority-authoring-plays.test.ts`. Do NOT touch tsconfig, do NOT rewrite import extensions, do NOT touch unrelated files, do NOT touch the validator/wrap-up.md or any shipped product contract.

GUARDRAIL: if any failure turns out to reveal a REAL product bug that would require reverting a shipped contract or a design judgment (rather than conforming a stale consumer/test), STOP and report it in your handoff instead of forcing the test green.

ACCEPTANCE: `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` ALL green (report the final pass counts), and `node scripts/check-topology.mjs` green. The 31 UI typecheck errors will remain (separate atom) — that is expected; do not attempt them here. Report each command you ran and its result, and confirm the diff is limited to the six allowlisted paths.

REPAIR MAIN, part 2 of 2 (founder-authorized): fix the 31 `pnpm -w typecheck` errors so the workspace typecheck is fully green. This is a tsconfig-WIRING fix ONLY. Do NOT change any UI source files, do NOT rewrite import extensions, do NOT add JSON import attributes.

CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSE (verified — do not re-investigate): the UI package is already typechecked correctly by its OWN dual config — `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui typecheck` (= `tsc -p packages/ui/tsconfig.json && tsc -p packages/ui/tsconfig.app.json`) reports 0 errors. `tsconfig.app.json` covers `src/renderer`, `src/main`, `src/preload`, `tests` under `moduleResolution: Bundler` + `allowImportingTsExtensions: true`, so the `.ts`-extension and JSON imports in the UI are INTENTIONAL and correct for the vite/Electron build. The 31 errors come ONLY from the ROOT `tsconfig.json`, whose `include: ["packages/*/src/**/*.ts", ...]` glob scoops up `packages/ui/src/**` and re-typechecks it under NodeNext (the wrong config for Bundler code). The UI SOURCE IS CORRECT.

FIX (two files only):
1. ROOT `tsconfig.json` — stop the root typecheck from compiling UI source under NodeNext. Add `"exclude": ["packages/ui/**"]` (the UI is covered by its own package-level typecheck). Keep the root glob covering all NON-UI packages (core, daemon, adapters, personas, etc.) exactly as before — do not narrow coverage of those.
2. ROOT `package.json` `typecheck` script — so `pnpm -w typecheck` still covers the UI via its CORRECT configs, chain in the UI's own typecheck: change `"tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json"` to `"tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json && pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui typecheck"` (or an equivalent that runs both `packages/ui/tsconfig.json` and `tsconfig.app.json`). The UI must still be typechecked by the workspace typecheck — just under its own configs, not NodeNext.

HARD SCOPE — final `git diff` may touch ONLY: `tsconfig.json` (root) and `package.json` (root). ZERO changes to any file under `packages/ui/**` (no source, no tsconfig edits there — they are already correct). Confirm with `git diff --stat` that only those two root files changed.

ACCEPTANCE (report each command + result):
- `pnpm -w typecheck` → 0 errors, fully green (this now covers non-UI packages under NodeNext AND the UI under its own dual config).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui typecheck` → still green (unchanged behavior).
- All suites still green: `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/adapters test`.
- `node scripts/check-topology.mjs` → green.
- Confirm NO file under `packages/ui/**` was modified.

GUARDRAIL: if excluding `packages/ui/**` from the root turns out to drop typecheck coverage of any non-UI source, or if `pnpm -w typecheck` does not end fully green with the two-file change, STOP and report the exact remaining errors rather than editing UI source to force it green.
